characters.name: open/close all folders
characters.description: Adaptational Nationality: Is Britishin his original iteration.In both the pilot and the series he's an American Jew (though his dad is still British). Ambiguously Jewish: He has a Jewish last name, and his mother has the stereotypicalJewish Motherpersonality and voice. Audience Surrogate: He's the POV character and the one asking most of the questions. Book Dumb: A mild example. Although Oscar is not generally presented as unintelligent, he seems to havesometrouble with reading and spelling, as mentioned in "Pajamas Party" and "Midnight Quittance". Everyone Has Standards: In "It's My Party", while he doesn't like Susie, he believes no one deserves to be alone on their birthday, even if it is Susie. This is because no one, including Hedgehog who had other plans, attended his fifth birthday party.

characters.name: Oscar Peltzer
characters.description: Adaptational Nationality: Is Britishin his original iteration.In both the pilot and the series he's an American Jew (though his dad is still British). Ambiguously Jewish: He has a Jewish last name, and his mother has the stereotypicalJewish Motherpersonality and voice. Audience Surrogate: He's the POV character and the one asking most of the questions. Book Dumb: A mild example. Although Oscar is not generally presented as unintelligent, he seems to havesometrouble with reading and spelling, as mentioned in "Pajamas Party" and "Midnight Quittance". Everyone Has Standards: In "It's My Party", while he doesn't like Susie, he believes no one deserves to be alone on their birthday, even if it is Susie. This is because no one, including Hedgehog who had other plans, attended his fifth birthday party.

characters.name: Hedgehog
characters.description: Abusive Parents: A fairly low-key example. In "Fuzzy Pink Time Babies", Hedgehog's father Jim forces her to enrol in business camp, despite the fact that this would tear her away from Oscar. Hedgehog is reduced to tears at this prospect and eventually has to tell her father to back off. Meanwhile, in "Momsoon", Hedgehog's mother calls in on Hedgehog's radio show to harangue her about how Hedgehog is not living up to her ridiculously high standards. Hedgehog reacts by asserting her autonomy as a separate individual. It's made clear that Hedgehog's parents want her to be successful, but their methods leave their daughter extremely unhappy. The Ace: Despite being normal with no magic powers like the counselors, she's slightly better than anyone else in any other activity. Boyish Short Hair: Adds to herViewer Gender Confusion. A Dog Named "Dog": Hedgehog is a hedgehog. Notably, she's the only camper who has this kind of name. On that note... Dub Name Change: ...her name varies depending on the language being spoken; it's "Scricciola" in the Italian dub, for instance. This is a play on "riccio", the Italian word for "hedgehog". Other dubs are more direct, such as the Spanish version, which gives her name as "Erizo" - literally, "hedgehog".Some dubs, e.g. the German one, don't translate her name.

characters.name: Max
characters.description: A laid back bat and general cool guy. He sometimes hangs out with Oscar and Hedgehog.His magical identity is a vampire. Adaptational Nice Guy: In the pilot, Max is a generic cool kid who thinks Oscar is a baby. He's a lot nicer in the series, and he generally has a good opinion of Oscar. Adaptational Species Change: Max was a badger in the pilot, but is a bat in the show. Bat Out of Hell: Usually aNice Guybut gets pegged into a villainous vampire role by the cursed video game. Big Eater: Downplayed. While he isn't shown eating a ton of food, he is shown snacking on something in most of his appearances.

characters.name: Pepper Corn
characters.description: Actor Allusion: Sam Lavagnino, who voices Pepper, also voiced Baby Panda and Baby Grizz in the pilot episode ofWe Bare Bears. A Day in the Limelight: "Pepper's Blanket is Missing" and "Pepper and the Fog". The Baby of the Bunch: Pepper is the youngest male among the main campers.Word of Godhas revealed that his age is nine, though this is upped to ten in "Pepper and the Fog". Expy: Of Linus van Pelt fromPeanuts. Hidden Depths: Pepper shows in-depth knowledge of art theory in "Puff Paint" and is shown to love reading in "The Library".

characters.name: Lucy Thompson
characters.description: Bookworm: She's usually seen reading books. Deadpan Snarker: She usually makes snarky comments about the matter of the moment. Expy: Has a slightly similar facial appearance to the Read family fromArthur. She's even got Arthur's glasses and DW's hairstyle. Tomboy and Girly Girl: Seems to be the girly girl to Alexa's tomboy.

characters.name: Oliver
characters.description: The Generic Guy: Besides the fact that he plays musical instruments, there is really nothing about his personality that makes him stand out among the cast. Green Thumb: In "Pepper's Blanket Is Missing", he is shown growing musical notes in a piano patch. Irony: Despite being a musician, in "Oscar & Hedgehog's Melody", he does tarot card reading at the talent show.

characters.name: Alexa Mongello
characters.description: Tomboy: She's shown to enjoy doing sports. Tomboy and Girly Girl: Seems to be the tomboy to Lucy's girly girl. She's also the tomboy to her little sister Lem's girly girl. Tomboy with a Girly Streak: In "Oscar & Hedgehog's Melody", her talent for the talent show is knitting. Tomboyish Baseball Cap: She's usually wearing a red beanie, but in "Chocolate Money Badgers", she wore a red cap for the entire episode before going back to wearing her beanie in later episodes. Tomboyish Ponytail: She's a tomboy who wears her hair in a ponytail.

characters.name: Lem Mongello
characters.description: The Baby of the Bunch: She's the youngest female and the overall youngest among the main campers.Word of Godhas revealed that her age is five. Girlish Pigtails: Fitting, since she's the youngest of the group. Punny Name: Lemon Jello. Tomboy and Girly Girl: She's the girly girl to her big sister Alexa's tomboy.

characters.name: All Three
characters.description: The Ageless:All of the three Summer Camp Island witches are revealed to be this. Susie has been around since at least 1892 and Alice and Betsy has been around since at least 1972. "Light as a Feather" reveals Susie and Alice were in the same witches class together, along with Ramona. In "Just You and Me", Susie reveals she's 400 years-old. Bizarre Belching: Since the three witchesbecame immortal resp. unaging, their memories and regrets sometimes slip out when they burp (the more traumatizing ones stick around, the less severe ones evaporate). These "burp ghosts" are also, according to Ramona, "purepast" (likely due to theimmortality/unagingof the burper) andkillJabberwocks, who are "purefuture" (because theyguard the time stream i.e. the future by trying to destroy anyone who enters "frozen time").Mildred, Susie's sister, who is also animmortal witch,fartsghosts. Mildred, Susie's sister, who is also animmortal witch,fartsghosts. Cheaters Never Prosper: Susie and the witches (but mostly Susie) try to cheat at softball to get out of a chore. Oscar holds out on cheating for a while but starts when it's clear that they aren't going to have a fair game unless they do. In the end Oscar realizes that things have spiraled out of control and calls things off, and all three witches have to do the chore anyway. Eye of Newt: Some of their spells require strange ingredients.

characters.name: SusieMcCallister
characters.description: Affably Evil:Invoked. Her speech to new campers sounds rather threatening despite the ostensible fact that it's supposed to be inviting; the Sun itself points out that "it gets scarier every year". When compared to the other two witches, yes, Susie is generally mean in personality, but she isn't truly evil. Affectionate Nickname: Susie, along with Alice, calls herself "potato". The Ageless:Susie has apparently been fifteen since atleast1892. She apparently chose to stay fifteen while her best friend Ramona chose to age. Alpha Bitch: Susie is this among the witches, and is mean to everyone else. Ambiguously Gay: Though never stated outright, her feelings towards Ramonadefinitelycan be read as being romantic in nature.

characters.name: Alice Fefferman
characters.description: Angry Dance: When she's frustrated no one came to Susie's party, she gets Oscar and Hedgehog to make it right by breaking out hermost threatening dance moves. Ambiguously Brown: She has brown fur. Beware the Silly Ones: She's a flightlyGirly Girl- who's obsessed with turning monsters into babies against their will. Additionally, she's the most powerful of the witches, according to Betsy, who cannot undo her spell on the monsters. Cuteness Proximity: Alice is tagged out of the softball game because she stops to pet a cute kitty. She thinks it's worth it. Fainting Seer:She can receive visions of the future, but when she does, her pupils seem to disappear, indicating that she loses consciousness or at least enters a state of trance when she receives a prophecy.

characters.name: Betsy Spellman
characters.description: Bookworm: She is often seen reading books that usually relates to their current situation. Cool Big Sis: She's this among the campers, especially towards Hedgehog when Hedgehog revealed that she has been teaching her magic in secret andgave her comfort and advice when they were werewolves and after Max rejects her love confession in "Spell Crusher". Nice Gal: She's the nicest camp counselor and is willing to help the campers. She's also proud of the fact that she's the nicest of the three counselors. Not So Above It All: Despite being the nicest witch, she will still join in with their mischief against the campers. Though Susie forces her to do so with blackmail or threats of violence. Our Werewolves Are Different:She's revealed to be this near the end of "Hedgehog Werewolf", and Susie is completely unaware of this until the episode “Just You and me”.

characters.name: Ramona
characters.description: Big Good: She's the head witch of the coven and leads everyone to try to save magic. Mayfly–December Friendship: With Susie, who is immortal, which prevents her from going into Frozen Time.The finale turns it into aMay December Friendshipwhen Time relieves Susie's immortality and they reunite. Morality Chain: To Susie, who probably would've descended toBig Badstatus if it wasn't for their friendship and she's usually the one who stops Susie from going too far.

characters.name: Howard
characters.description: A tall, purple monster who wears glasses and has a single pink horn. Horned Humanoid: He has a single pink horn on top of his head. Smart People Wear Glasses: Howard wears glasses and he's one of the smart monsters, possibly theirlancertoo.

characters.name: Ava
characters.description: A tall, white monster with black spots and black ears and a snout. She can take and print Polaroids with her eyes and mouth respectively.

characters.name: Mortimer
characters.description: A tall, light blue monster with rabbit-like ears. The Leader: He's revealed as this to the monsters, having brought them together at Harvard with Oscar's help. He also represents the monsters as their "Prime Monister" in "Meeting of the Minds".

characters.name: Melvin
characters.description: A short, orange monster. Gag Nose: His nose is shaped like a heart.

characters.name: Blanche
characters.description: A tall, brown monster who has a door in front of her body. Horned Humanoid: She has two white horns.

characters.name: Margot
characters.description: A medium-sized, purple monster with wings. Scary Librarian: She's revealed to be a librarian at the library of the camp, and she will get scary mad if anyone makes too much noise. Winged Humanoid: She has tiny wings on her body. These are shown to keep her aloft, at least when she's in a younger form in "Monster Babies".

characters.name: Stuart
characters.description: Obstructive Bureaucrat: He works as this at the Bank of Heartforde and is just as obnoxious. He also served as a secret society proctor during his time at Harvard University. Sitcom Arch-Nemesis: He's this to Hedgehog due to fighting with her over a dictionary. Token Evil Teammate: So far, he's the only monster who is known to be a completeJerkass.

characters.name: Barb Goldberg
characters.description: The head of the elves who runs the Soundhouse on the island. Berserk Button: Oscar learns to not mention her age after she slaps his face twiceclaiming there was a mosquito on his face. E.T. Gave Us Wi-Fi: She created "putting pants on one leg at a time" and the "crush-fession". Like a Son to Me: In the episode "When Harry Met Barry" Barb says to Oscar and Hedgehog that she likes to think of them as her 'camp kids'. Rube Goldberg Device: Most of her inventions are these, especially the Soundhouse, but she calls them a "Barb Goldberg Machine".

characters.name: Pajamas
characters.description: Oscar's pajamas that come to life when she's on Summer Camp Island. Ambiguous Gender: In "Pajama Pajimjams", Pajamas is referred to with he/him pronouns, but in Season 2, Pajamas is later referred with she/her pronouns in "The Haunted Campfire" and "Pajamas Party". Cheerful Child: She behaves and sounds like a friendly, energetic child. Keet: She is very energetic. Living Clothes: She's Oscar's favorite set of pajamas. She came alive at the camp.

characters.name: The Sun and Moon
characters.description: A living sun and moon that can only talk when they're at Summer Camp Island. Demoted to Extra: The Sun and Moon had some speaking roles in Season 1, but they haven't said a single word in Season 2. I Just Want to Have Friends: The Moon just wants to have friends since most people are asleep when he comes. Light Is Not Good: The Sun throws a hissy fit over not having her hairstyle appreciated, making everyone suffer in heat. She also doesn't value friendship as much as the Moon does. Lunacy/The Power of the Sun: Naturally. Their supernatural powers are made very clear.

characters.name: Aliens
characters.description: Diminutive, furry aliens that serve as assistants to the witches and the campers. The King and Puddles are two notable members of this species. Emotion Eater: Emotions are limited on the Alien planet, and so all of them have to share the resultant energy; however, if even one Alien does not express their emotions, that Alien will end up hoarding all emotions on the planet until they can express them in some way. Puddle ends up doing just this in "Feeling Spacey" when they struggle with confessing their love for the King. Flying Saucer: Their primary mode of transportation. No Biological Sex: The Aliens seem to be masculine, since they have a king, but no gender-specific pronouns are used to describe them. Really 700 Years Old: The King is said to be over a millennium old in "Feeling Spacey".

characters.name: Yetis
characters.description: Yetis that appear on the island. Bigfoot, Sasquatch, and Yeti: Of the snow variety. Chameleon Camouflage: They have the ability to do this. Horned Humanoid: Yetis will get their horns when they become an adult. Intelligible Unintelligible: While the audience cannot understand them, everyone in-universe can.

characters.name: Monster Under the Bed
characters.description: A strange translucent monster that lives under the bed in Hedgehog's cabin. Deadpan Snarker: His usual response to the other characters. Fanboy: He's revealed to be this to Hedgehog in "Campers Above the Bed".

characters.name: Dr. P. Shark
characters.description: A shark who lives in the pool by Oscar's cabin. The Shrink: He's a licensed therapist, but on his business card he's a "licensed ther-pyst". Talking Animal: He's a more-or-less normal shark, except he can talk. Threatening Shark: Subverted, as his vocation can attest.

characters.name: MildredMcCallister
characters.description: 11th-Hour Ranger:When Susie becomes trapped in theMetaphysical Reserveand all the witches out of commission, the campers resort calling her to the island to save Susie. Blessed with Suck: Quite literally from birth, Mildred was a magical savant butshe has trouble controlling her magicand usually conjures up frogs unintentionally. Big Ol' Unibrow: Sports a quite impressive one.

characters.name: The Rascals
characters.description: Berserk Button: When the wolf arrives to collect Mildred's magic and cannot find the jars it has been stored in, his calm demeanor evaporates and he becomes furious with her, vowing to return and get it. Jackass Genie: Of a sort. Their aim is to devour magic by first convincing the user that to be rid of it is what they desire most or offering what they desire most in exchange for the magic. Alternatively, they can take what you adore most if magic is not given to them. Magic Eater: In this world, magic takes the form of delicious jelly and it's a Rascal's favorite and most sought-after food. Barb's fake pools don't satiate their hunger. Meaningless Villain Victory: Ultimately, they do indeed succeed at eating all of Mildred's magic, it just turns out that eating the magic of a witch with severePower Incontinencewasn't the best idea as it incapacitates them. More than Mind Control: Having been tricked into sleeping forever by Barb, they use their time in the dreamscape to become very good atAstral Projectionand plant the idea to enter the Metaphysical Reserve in Susie's head so they can escape.
